trivia_bullets.text: Green Instant uncommon (MKM, no. 161, February 9, 2024), illustrated by Jesper Ejsing. Card name literalizes the idiom 'to gain an advantage' — a centaur's leg serves as a step for a smaller companion.
+1/+1 per creature you control plus reach until EOT; scales with board presence (5 creatures = +5/+5 plus reach). Particularly potent in token-flood strategies.

"Apologies for using you like a stepladder. On the other hand, thank you, you're a wonderful stepladder!" Speaker is unnamed; scene context (elf-eared figure reaching the shelf) suggests she is the speaker addressing the centaur.

Artist Jesper Ejsing is a Danish fantasy illustrator who has contributed MTG artwork since Lorwyn; maintains an active blog on Muddy Colors discussing creative process. Credited with at least two cards in MKM.
